Please refer to below proposal to minimize the idle time of Crane (we discussed it to Mr. Lakhveer)

*For UPC Deliveries (if 2x Trips per Trailer)*

25/10/22 PM 3:04 - DaN: Dear Base Operations Team,

Good day.
Thank you for your continued support and guidance.

We hope this message finds you well.

We are writing to formally request the immediate replacement of the currently supplied 15-ton forklift (HPFL-169)  in use at SCT Old Yard & VP-24. 
Samsung C&T operations involve handling highly fragile (HITACHI & SIEMENS Boxes with impact indicators) and high-value project cargoes, and it is critical that all equipment used meets the highest standards of safety and performance.

Unfortunately, the existing forklift HPFL-169 has demonstrated poor performance during lifting operations. It exhibits pronounced jerking and bucking motions, which strongly suggest a serious mechanical fault. 
This equipment compromises the safety of SCT lifting operations and poses a significant risk of damage to our sensitive materials.

Additionally, the oversized fork tines are severely limiting our ability to offload and maneuver cargo efficiently. 
This has resulted in substantial delays and increased the risk of unsafe handling practices.

Given the urgency and importance of maintaining safe and efficient operations, we kindly request your immediate support in providing a suitable replacement unit that aligns with our operational requirements.
Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. We look forward to your swift response.

26/3/16 PM 3:49 - DaN: *Observation Summary* 

*Sunday, 15 March 2026*
 > Delivery of 2-nos - MAU-ACCU  - OS0000457/MIRFA SITE delivery from SKM was scheduled for Sunday, 15 March 2026 at 0900 hrs, with offloading planned at the CACHA parking area.
 > Despite equipment and manpower being arranged in advance, the SKM driver did not arrive as scheduled. 
 > MIRFA Logistics personnel sent an email requesting confirmation of the rescheduled delivery time; however, no response was received from SKM.

*On 16 March 2026, 0930hrs,*
 > SKM driver arrived without prior notice and proceeded directly to the CICPA Gate and not to Cacha parking area (designated location) without informing the MIRFA Logistics Team.
 > This resulted in an escalation between the driver and CICPA authorities. 
 > As a consequence, MIRFA Logistics personnel and Admin Team representative were summoned by the CICPA Authority Captain to explain the situation. 
 > *Despite MIRFA Team providing documentation showing that the SKM driver was not scheduled for that day and the delivery location is not inside the MIRFA Site premises, CICPA insisted on conducting further investigation.* 

*CICPA AUTORITY INVESTIGATION*
 > CICPA Authority personnel then visited MIRFA Site to inspect MIRFA Logistics phone and laptop. 
 > Additional checks were conducted on other MIRFA Site team members. 
 > No explanation was provided by CICPA regarding the reason for these searches.

*Impact on MIRFA Site Due to SKM’s Negligence and Failure to Follow Logistics Instructions* 
 > MIRFA Logistics CICPA card was confiscated for further investigation, resulting in his restricted access to MIRFA Site premises. 
 > Work stoppage occurred to accommodate CICPA Authority inspections. 
 > CICPA Authority Captain ordered a four-day work suspension to finalize the investigation. - currently under review by SCT and CICPA Authority.

*Conclusion*
 > The incident has caused significant operational disruption and reputational risk to MIRFA Site. 
 > The lack of coordination and non-compliance from SKM directly contributed to the escalation with CICPA authorities. 

Further corrective action and preventive measures may be required to avoid recurrence.
